subject: PATIENT
heading: No Heading Found - Table__page_no__3
text: header_1: :: header_2: was positive, culture later positive for e. coli. sputum culture also positive for e. coli. antibiotics were changed to ancef. ammonia level 124 on admission with lactic acid 2.8. lactulose was initiated. ct of head unremarkable. patient also diagnosed with bilateral pneumonia. required levophed secondary to hypotension secondary to the sepsis. diagnosed with type ii mi. cardiology was consulted. urine culture positive for amphetamines, marijuana and oxycodone. bilateral lower extremity arterial dopplers with mild left lower extremity arterial disease. echocardiogram showed ejection fraction 25% with moderate pericardial effusion without tamponade. patient was diagnosed with new onset atrial fibrillation/flutter with rvr with episodes of bradycardia. eliquis was initiated for anticoagulation. synthroid was increased with tsh at 41. concern for myxedema coma. discussed potential pacemaker in the future. gastroenterology was consulted. entresto, aldactone, jardiance and lasix were added per cardiology recommendations. follow-up echocardiogram did show improved pericardial effusion. ct of spine showed 1.4 cm round nodule at left parotid gland, recommend follow-up imaging in the outpatient setting. ultrasound of liver showed no obvious blood clot, cirrhotic liver morphology with small to moderate ascites, abnormal:: header_3: follow up with [provider_name] when [encounter_date] 02:00 pm edt:: header_4: why: cardio where: [address]

text: header_1: :: header_2: was positive, culture later positive for e. coli. sputum culture also positive for e. coli. antibiotics were changed to ancef. ammonia level 124 on admission with lactic acid 2.8. lactulose was initiated. ct of head unremarkable. patient also diagnosed with bilateral pneumonia. required levophed secondary to hypotension secondary to the sepsis. diagnosed with type ii mi. cardiology was consulted. urine culture positive for amphetamines, marijuana and oxycodone. bilateral lower extremity arterial dopplers with mild left lower extremity arterial disease. echocardiogram showed ejection fraction 25% with moderate pericardial effusion without tamponade. patient was diagnosed with new onset atrial fibrillation/flutter with rvr with episodes of bradycardia. eliquis was initiated for anticoagulation. synthroid was increased with tsh at 41. concern for myxedema coma. discussed potential pacemaker in the future. gastroenterology was consulted. entresto, aldactone, jardiance and lasix were added per cardiology recommendations. follow-up echocardiogram did show improved pericardial effusion. ct of spine showed 1.4 cm round nodule at left parotid gland, recommend follow-up imaging in the outpatient setting. ultrasound of liver showed no obvious blood clot, cirrhotic liver morphology with small to moderate ascites, abnormal:: header_3: follow up with [provider_name] when [encounter_date] 02:00 pm edt:: header_4: why: cardio where: [address]

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__14
text: history of present illness interim history: 80 year old female presents with c/o follow-up she has only been home for less than 48 hours. she still feels very weak. she is walking with a walker. she has been getting up and moving around. she has been getting a little lightheaded, but not terrible. her son and daughter are helping organize her medications and are helping with her care right now. she is tolerating them very well compared to what she has been on in the past, especially the diuretics. aultman home health was out yesterday. she is having at least 2 bm's a day and they did up her lactulose and she is now having more and her mentation seems to be better. she has follow up scheduled with cardiology and gi. we reviewed the hospitalization and all questions answered. she is urinating well. bruising is resolving on her knees. biggest complaint today is right shoulder pain that has hurt since she was in the hospital. it hurts more on the posterior aspect and hurts when she uses it and touches that area. transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name] [encounter_date]-[encounter_date] transition from skilled nursing facility woodlawn [encounter_date]-[encounter_date] transition to home (own) admission date [encounter_date] 30 day readmission no number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. o discharge date [encounter_date]

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__14
text: discharge summary report received yes per hospital records on [encounter_date]: hospital course 80-year-old female with a past medical history of hemochromatosis, liver disease, hepatitis c via blood transfusion, hfpef, atrial fibrillation, pulmonary hypertension, adhd/anxiety who initially presented to the icu on [encounter_date] for metabolic encephalopathy. patient was intubated in the ed for her encephalopathy and inability to protect airway. on presentation she was found to have a uti with small blood, nitrates, 4+ bacteria and ammonia was significantly elevated at 124 lactic acid was elevated 2.8. troponin was also elevated at 1393. chest x-ray demonstrated multifocal patchy bilateral interstitial/alveolar opacities with focal right midlung consolidation concerning for multifocal pneumonia as well. head ct was negative for acute findings. ct spine showed a 1.4 cm round nodule with left parotid gland. urine drug screen was positive for amphetamines, marijuana and oxycodone. patient was eventually transferred out of the icu and rocephin for pneumonia and azithromycin was discontinued given atypicals were negative. patient did require pressors in the icu for hypotension and was also started on tube feeds initially. echocardiogram demonstrated ef of 30 to 35% with akinesis of the apical/mid myocardium. she also had a moderate to large pericardial effusion with fibrinous appearance of fluid. patient did have a brief episode of atrial flutter which spontaneously converted back to sinus rhythm. patient was weaned off ventilator and off pressors and cultures demonstrated pansensitive e. coli in both respiratory and urine culture and was transition to cefazolin. patient received 13 days total of antibiotics. gi

text: discharge summary report received yes per hospital records on [encounter_date]: hospital course 80-year-old female with a past medical history of hemochromatosis, liver disease, hepatitis c via blood transfusion, hfpef, atrial fibrillation, pulmonary hypertension, adhd/anxiety who initially presented to the icu on [encounter_date] for metabolic encephalopathy. patient was intubated in the ed for her encephalopathy and inability to protect airway. on presentation she was found to have a uti with small blood, nitrates, 4+ bacteria and ammonia was significantly elevated at 124 lactic acid was elevated 2.8. troponin was also elevated at 1393. chest x-ray demonstrated multifocal patchy bilateral interstitial/alveolar opacities with focal right midlung consolidation concerning for multifocal pneumonia as well. head ct was negative for acute findings. ct spine showed a 1.4 cm round nodule with left parotid gland. urine drug screen was positive for amphetamines, marijuana and oxycodone. patient was eventually transferred out of the icu and rocephin for pneumonia and azithromycin was discontinued given atypicals were negative. patient did require pressors in the icu for hypotension and was also started on tube feeds initially. echocardiogram demonstrated ef of 30 to 35% with akinesis of the apical/mid myocardium. she also had a moderate to large pericardial effusion with fibrinous appearance of fluid. patient did have a brief episode of atrial flutter which spontaneously converted back to sinus rhythm. patient was weaned off ventilator and off pressors and cultures demonstrated pansensitive e. coli in both respiratory and urine culture and was transition to cefazolin. patient received 13 days total of antibiotics. gi

text: discharge summary report received yes per hospital records on [encounter_date]: hospital course 80-year-old female with a past medical history of hemochromatosis, liver disease, hepatitis c via blood transfusion, hfpef, atrial fibrillation, pulmonary hypertension, adhd/anxiety who initially presented to the icu on [encounter_date] for metabolic encephalopathy. patient was intubated in the ed for her encephalopathy and inability to protect airway. on presentation she was found to have a uti with small blood, nitrates, 4+ bacteria and ammonia was significantly elevated at 124 lactic acid was elevated 2.8. troponin was also elevated at 1393. chest x-ray demonstrated multifocal patchy bilateral interstitial/alveolar opacities with focal right midlung consolidation concerning for multifocal pneumonia as well. head ct was negative for acute findings. ct spine showed a 1.4 cm round nodule with left parotid gland. urine drug screen was positive for amphetamines, marijuana and oxycodone. patient was eventually transferred out of the icu and rocephin for pneumonia and azithromycin was discontinued given atypicals were negative. patient did require pressors in the icu for hypotension and was also started on tube feeds initially. echocardiogram demonstrated ef of 30 to 35% with akinesis of the apical/mid myocardium. she also had a moderate to large pericardial effusion with fibrinous appearance of fluid. patient did have a brief episode of atrial flutter which spontaneously converted back to sinus rhythm. patient was weaned off ventilator and off pressors and cultures demonstrated pansensitive e. coli in both respiratory and urine culture and was transition to cefazolin. patient received 13 days total of antibiotics. gi

subject: PATIENT
heading: No Heading Found__page_no__17
text: treatment :: 1. other cirrhosis of liver refill spironolactone tablet, 25 mg, 1 tab(s), orally, once a day, 90 days, 90 tablet, refills 3 notes: currently compensated. she is tolerating lasix and spironolactone and is taking it. does need midodrine to help keep her bp up, but hopefully we will be able to wean that out as she stabilizes over the next several months. discussed with [patient_name] and [patient_name] (son and dtr) to monitor for any brain fog/beginning confusion and to ensure she is having 3-5 bm's per day to stave off metabolic encephalopathy. they are scheduled to see [provider_name] in follow up. they will reach out to him re: rifaximin as it is incredibly expensive to see if there is an alternative he wants to use or whether to just stick with the lactulose. discussed why people with cirrhosis have screening egd's and what the potential outcomes are. 2. chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, unspecified copd type notes: breathing seems to be very stable. okay for albuterol prn

text: treatment :: 1. other cirrhosis of liver refill spironolactone tablet, 25 mg, 1 tab(s), orally, once a day, 90 days, 90 tablet, refills 3 notes: currently compensated. she is tolerating lasix and spironolactone and is taking it. does need midodrine to help keep her bp up, but hopefully we will be able to wean that out as she stabilizes over the next several months. discussed with [patient_name] and [patient_name] (son and dtr) to monitor for any brain fog/beginning confusion and to ensure she is having 3-5 bm's per day to stave off metabolic encephalopathy. they are scheduled to see [provider_name] in follow up. they will reach out to him re: rifaximin as it is incredibly expensive to see if there is an alternative he wants to use or whether to just stick with the lactulose. discussed why people with cirrhosis have screening egd's and what the potential outcomes are. 2. chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, unspecified copd type notes: breathing seems to be very stable. okay for albuterol prn
